key_insight: Scaling up GPT-style language models delays but does not eliminate plasticity loss, and the onset appears to grow sublinearly with model size.
engineering_relevance: For continual learning in large language models, parameter scaling alone is not a complete fix; training strategy and adaptation mechanisms still matter.
method: Trained GPT-style Transformer models from 5M to 314M non-embedding parameters on a multilingual continual learning setup.
Measured plasticity loss using deterioration on a held-out Vietnamese probing task.
Compared continual learning with stationary multilingual training to test whether plasticity loss also appears without abrupt task changes.
evidence_signal: Across all tested model sizes, the abstract reports measurable plasticity loss on the held-out Vietnamese probe, with the onset following a sublinear scaling law.
watch_item: Bigger models may postpone visible plasticity loss, but long training can still reduce adaptability, so scaling should not be assumed to preserve lifelong learning.
paper_title: Can Scale Save Us From Plasticity Loss in Large Language Models?
